subtitle: Physics simulation and scientific computing for lattice field theory
description: Physics simulation and scientific computing environment for lattice field theory, fermions, and gauge fields. Run and manage simulations, list saved environments, and create new physics simulations with configurable modules.
mcp_connector_instructions: Deploy the MCP server to a publicly accessible HTTPS URL (e.g. Cloud Run, Fly.io, or use ngrok for testing). Then in the submission form, set Connector URL to: https://<your-domain>/mcp

tools.name: list_simulations
tools.title: List simulations
tools.description: Returns the user's saved simulations without modifying them.

tools.name: create_simulation
tools.title: Create simulation
tools.description: Creates a new physics simulation with the given parameters.

indirect: I want to see what I've run before

test_prompts_and_responses.expected_response_summary: App calls list_simulations and returns a list of saved simulations (or empty list with count 0). No side effects.
Same as above; list_simulations returns simulations without modifying anything.

test_prompts_and_responses.prompt: Create a new simulation with env_id demo_env and modules FERMION
test_prompts_and_responses.expected_response_summary: App calls create_simulation with env_id and modules; returns confirmation of created simulation.
App should NOT be triggered (negative test); ChatGPT answers without using BestBrain.

screenshots_note: Upload screenshots of the app in use (required dimensions per dashboard).
checklist_before_submit: MCP server deployed at public HTTPS URL (e.g. https://your-app.run.app/mcp).
CSP defined; no localhost in production.
Demo account (no MFA) if app requires auth.
